issue.title: `pnpm create astro` fails silently on Linux when path contains non-ASCII characters (Unicode NFC vs NFD normalization bug)

labels.name: - P4: important
labels.description: Violate documented behavior or significantly impacts performance (priority)

labels.name: triage: fix verified
labels.description: Reporter confirmed the triage bot fix works

issue.body: ### Astro Info ```block Astro v4.x (Latest via pnpm create astro@latest) OS: Manjaro Linux (Kernel v6.x) Package Manager: pnpm v11.13.0 Shell: zsh ``` ### If this issue only occurs in one browser, which browser is a problem? Not a browser issue. It occurs during the CLI scaffolding process in the terminal. ### Describe the Bug When running `pnpm create astro@latest` inside a directory path that contains non-ASCII/Unicode characters (e.g., Turkish characters like `ü` in `/home/saqut/Masaüstü/`), the installation script finishes with a successful exit code ("Project initialized!"), but the target directory remains empty except for root monorepo files. The `src/` folder, `package.json`, and `astro.config.mjs` are missing. By tracing the system calls using `strace`, we uncovered that the scaffolding tool uses **NFD (Decomposed Unicode)** format for path names, while Linux (ext4) expects **NFC (Canonical Decomposition)**. As seen in the `strace` log below, Node/Astro tries to resolve the path using NFD components (`Masau\314\210stu\314\210` instead of `Masaüstü`): ```text 86653 openat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/saqut/Masau\314\210stu\314\210/saqutcompiler/webside/deneme/astro.config.mjs", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC, 0664) = 31 86656 openat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/saqut/Masau\314\210stu\314\210/saqutcompiler/webside/deneme/package.json", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC, 0664) = 32 ### What's the expected result? The installer should sanitize, normalize, or preserve the exact Unicode normalization form (NFC) used by the host OS filesystem, ensuring that files like package.json and the src/ directory are successfully written to the correct physical path. ### Link to Minimal Reproducible Example https://github.com/withastro/astro#NFC-NFD-Unicode-Path-Bug ### Participation - [ ] I am willing to submit a pull request for this issue.

comment.body:
As this is a bug with `modern-tar`, I'm already looking into it in this [PR](https://github.com/ayuhito/modern-tar/pull/151). Will give updates when I am fully satisfied with the solution and it is released.

I don't believe the current workaround solutions proposed for Astro is the securest or cleanest when it comes to long term maintenance.

issue.title: fix(sass): replace deprecated if() function with @if/@else blocks
issue.body:
Replace all inline Sass if() function calls with @if/@else directive blocks in mixins.scss. The if() function syntax is deprecated and will become an error in future Sass versions.

The refactored code maintains identical logic and output, verified by compiling both versions and diffing the CSS to confirm identical output.

Closes #1094

comment.body: Sorry for the delay in reviewing this! I had some pre-requisite things I wanted to do in this repository! Thank you!

issue.title: SixtyFour doesn't load with documented import
issue.body:
### Describe the bug

https://fontsource.org/fonts/sixtyfour/install suggests I can import the font via

```js
// Supports only weight 400
import '@fontsource-variable/sixtyfour';
```

However, this didn't load the font. Instead, I've been using the full stylesheet

```js
import "@fontsource-variable/sixtyfour/full.css";
```

I'm working on a site built with Astro, v.5.1.8

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Follow astro's font-loading steps here: https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/fonts/#using-fontsource

with the following export used for step 3

```js
import '@fontsource-variable/sixtyfour';
```

2. Use the font in any CSS rule



### Expected behavior

For the font to load whenever used

### Version

5.1.1

### OS

Mac

### Browser

N/A

### Additional context

I did notice that in the package on-disk, after installing, the `index.css` file, listed as the main export, is completely empty. The `full.css` and `bled.css` files are populated

payload.description: Self-host Open Source fonts in neatly bundled NPM packages.

issue.title: Please provide a TypeScript type declaration file in font packages
issue.body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Please include the file `index.d.css.ts` in generated NPM modules. This file can be empty, it just needs to exist.

This file helps with TypeScript projects that enable `noUncheckedSideEffectImports` option (docs: https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig/#noUncheckedSideEffectImports). When this option is enabled, TypeScript is unable to import untyped CSS files. This is documented in the issue: https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/62674

This `noUncheckedSideEffectImports` option is recommended by the TypeScript project and will become default enabled in future version.

### Describe the solution you'd like

When generating font packages, include an empty file: `index.d.css.ts` as a sibling to `index.css`.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I can workaround this by defining the types myself in a type declaration file, but this is tedious when using many fonts and would need to be repeated for every TypeScript project.

### Additional context

_No response_

issue.title: Website Redesign Tasklist
issue.body:
# Website Redesign

Fontsource has grown a lot and so have our needs. We're moving to a new website that aims to improve user experience and introduce new functionality that clears a lot of issues in the repository. 

We've got a bunch of new design mockups sponsored by the awesome folks at @jsdelivr which can be viewed at each issue below for their respective pages.

## Tech Stack

Since this is a significant change, we're moving away from our current Next.js setup and starting from scratch. We'll be relying on [Remix](https://remix.run/) + [Fly.io](https://fly.io/) for it's optimal SSR flexibility. Likewise, we'll be moving over to [Mantine](https://mantine.dev/) for its easy to use and accessible components.

## Task List

- [x] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/551
- [x] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/552
- [x] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/553
- [x] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/554
- [ ] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/555
- [x] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/556
- [x] https://github.com/fontsource/fontsource/issues/557

The full project board can be found here: [Fontsource Project Board](https://github.com/orgs/fontsource/projects/1)
You can follow the progress in our [Discord](https://discord.gg/pJgkn7xjAj) or website branch at [beta.fontsource.org](https://beta.fontsource.org/).
